message:
Use write operators instead of print operators

There are print functions and write operators. Updated terminology
in documentation text and code examples to use write_* operators.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -22,7 +22,7 @@ In both cases, you can only provide static header data.
 Use [`from_http`](/reference/operators/from_http) to perform HTTP requests or
 run an HTTP server. This operator automatically infers the format from the
 `Content-Type` header, defaulting to JSON. For sending, use
-[`save_http`](/reference/operators/save_http) with a print operator.
+[`save_http`](/reference/operators/save_http) with a write operator.
 
 ## Examples
 
@@ -45,6 +45,6 @@ from {
   x: 42,
   y: "foo",
 }
-print_json
+write_json
 save_http "http://example.com:8888/api", method="POST"
 ```

@@ -16,7 +16,7 @@ Use the IP address `0.0.0.0` to listen on all available network interfaces.
 
 Use [`from_udp`](/reference/operators/from_udp) to receive UDP datagrams as
 structured events containing message data and peer information. For sending, use
-[`save_udp`](/reference/operators/save_udp) with a print operator.
+[`save_udp`](/reference/operators/save_udp) with a write operator.
 
 ### Receive syslog messages over UDP
 
@@ -29,6 +29,6 @@ this = data.parse_syslog()
 
 ```tql
 from {message: "Tenzir"}
-print_ndjson
+write_ndjson
 save_udp "1.2.3.4:8080"
 ```
